mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS is positive, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential monetary policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:historical inverse correlation between low unemployment and future equity returns
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 6.0% and the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains exceptionally low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, and the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) during a post-recession recovery phase signals strengthening economic activity and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month growth exceeds 0.5% during a post-recession recovery, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand, which typically leads to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 900k units for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of those periods over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above a low threshold indicates monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:historical impact of rate hikes on equity markets
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% and S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of the subsequent periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but retail sales (RSAFS) are decelerating, it suggests a divergence where overall economic output is not translating into consumer spending, potentially signaling future weakness and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-period growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 0%
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is strong while RSAFS growth is negative, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 2-year rolling window.
